verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase, canonically in the mitochondrial matrix-facing F1 head. However, ectopic surface expression of ATP synthase components (including beta subunit) on tumor cells, hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and adipocytes is well documented, where it functions as a receptor for apoA-I, angiostatin, and others. This cancer/tissue-restricted ecto-ATP synthase pool has been targeted by antibodies, qualifying as cell-state-induced surface display.
